label: Precalculus
shortDescription: Advanced functions, trigonometry, complex numbers, and preparation for calculus.
modulesDescription: Read the precalculus chapters in order, or jump directly to the topic you need.
ogDescription: Free precalculus course covering functions, polynomials, exponentials, trigonometry, systems, complex numbers, and conics with detailed explanations and practice.
courseDescription: Master precalculus: functions and graphs, polynomial and rational functions, exponential and logarithmic functions, trigonometric functions, analytic trigonometry, systems and matrices, complex numbers and polar coordinates, sequences series and conics. Features ELI5 intuition, worked examples with steps and tables, common pitfalls, and high-quality practice questions.

topics.title: Functions & Graphs
topics.description: Function notation, domain and range, symmetry, inverses, composition, transformations, and piecewise definitions.

topics.title: Polynomial & Rational Functions
topics.description: Polynomial behavior, zeros and roots, rational functions, asymptotes, and partial fractions.

topics.title: Exponential & Logarithmic Functions
topics.description: Exponential and logarithmic rules, the natural log, logarithmic scales, and growth models.

topics.title: Trigonometric Functions
topics.description: Sine, cosine, tangent, the unit circle, and core trigonometric identities.

topics.title: Analytic Trigonometry
topics.description: Angle formulas, laws of sines and cosines, inverse trig, equations, and substitution preview.

topics.title: Systems & Matrices
topics.description: Linear systems, matrix arithmetic, determinants, inverses, and Gaussian elimination.

topics.title: Complex Numbers & Polar Coordinates
topics.description: Complex arithmetic, the complex plane, polar form, De Moivre's theorem, polar and parametric curves.

topics.title: Sequences, Series & Conics
topics.description: Sequences, arithmetic and geometric progressions, the binomial theorem, and conic sections.

topics.title: Introduction to Limits
topics.description: Limit notation and intuition as a bridge from precalculus into calculus.
